issue_title: Expose MaxUnpooling operator
issue_body:
MaxUnpooling operator API is not available despite its implementation [here](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/src/operator/nn/pool.h#L388). This was also discussed in [dmlc#640](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/issues/640) but Bilinear sampling was implemented in [dmlc#746](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/pull/746) which is *different*.

After that it'd easy to support it in Gluon. Related to [this feature request](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/issues/9905).

I have the same error as https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/issues/1104 but the proposed solutions (`make clean`/ `make clean_all` prior to built) do not work. Building the MXNet core shared library works fine on face value, but building the MXNet R binding/package fails.

The exact commands I use are:
```
rm mxnet/ -rf
git clone --recursive https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet.git mxnet --branch 0.11.0
cd mxnet/

cp make/config.mk .
echo "ADD_CFLAGS += -I/usr/include/openblas" >>config.mk
make -j $(nproc) USE_OPENCV=0 USE_BLAS=openblas USE_LAPACK=1
make rpkg

The error that I get is:

issue_title: Feature: Allow for Hosts to change IP (kafka-rolling-restart)
issue_body:
I have my Kafka cluster in the cloud, and I have two kinds of reboot:
- A soft reboot, which is just restarting my brokers
- A hard reboot, which terminates instances, and wait for new ones to come up (using a task, thanks for the doc!)

With the hard reboot, because the IP changes and the connection is terminated, the ssh Connection object will fail on the start_kafka command. 

So we need a new SSH connection to be instantiated before start_kafka command is executed to ensure we execute it against the right host.

issue_title: Incompatibilities with `five.pt`
issue_body:
As we notice into these issues (https://github.com/zopefoundation/five.pt/issues/5 and https://github.com/malthe/chameleon/issues/59) we have some incompatibilities with `five.pt` and `chameleon`.

In summary, `plone.app.imaging` is waiting for a `list` while `chameleon` uses a `tuple` instead as an speed optimization.

In this issue, we should make `plone.app.imaging` accept a `tuple` as an argument.

issue_title: Get impyla running with SSL on Python 3
issue_body: The new Python 3 support depends on `thriftpy`, which does not implement `TSSLSocket`.  This is necessary for supporting SSL connections.  Also see eleme/thriftpy#136.

A quick debugging session indicated that pythons `selectors.py` tries to squeeze out a file descriptor from the ZMQ Socket object by calling `fileno()`, but the object does not have this method. I monkey-patched something like this into it:

class Socket(_AsyncIO, _future._AsyncSocket):
    """Socket returning asyncio Futures for send/recv/poll methods."""

    _poller_class = Poller

    def fileno(self): 
        return self.FD
```

which resolved the issue. 

Any idea why this is not working out of the box? Any version mismatches?

issue_title: export common errors from the sops package

In userplex, I had to define a few custom errors that I think would be better placed in the sops package:
```go
const (
	errNotSopsFormat string = "sops metadata not found in input data"
	errInvalidMac    string = "failed to verify the integrity of the sops file (MAC mismatch)"
)
```
Maybe we need to rethink the error codes from the sops cmd line and export an error type that has both the error string and the code, similar to [os.PathError](https://golang.org/pkg/os/#PathError).

issue_title: Explore more than 50 epochs
issue_body:
An off-by one bug addressed in branch `restore-off-by-one` caused a re-start after a crash to train beyond epoch 50. As there were several re-starts we ended upon going to around epoch 53-54.

Upon going past epoch 50 we found that the validation WER continued to drop. Between epoch 50 and 53-54 it dropped a full percentage point.

Thus, it seems logical to officially run the system beyond epoch 50 to explore how the WER decreases in this region.

issue_title: ValidationError override multiple errors on the same field
issue_body:
Hi!

When you have a validation error with multiple errors on the same field, only the last message is kept.

Eg. On a subscription error you can have a xml like that:
```xml

<errors>
  <error field="subscription.account.billing_info.address1" symbol="empty">can't be empty</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.billing_info.city" symbol="empty">can't be empty</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.billing_info.country" symbol="empty">can't be empty</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.billing_info.first_name" symbol="blank">can't be blank</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.billing_info.last_name" symbol="blank">can't be blank</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.billing_info.number" symbol="required">is required</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.billing_info.zip" symbol="empty">can't be empty</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.account_code" symbol="blank">can't be blank</error>
  <error field="subscription.account.account_code" symbol="invalid">is invalid</error>
  <error field="subscription.plan_code" symbol="invalid">is invalid</error>
  <error field="subscription.unit_amount_in_cents" symbol="not_a_number">is not a number</error>
</errors>
```
And the python representation of the error is:
```python

In [98]: try:
    ...:     recurly.Subscription(account=recurly.Account(billing_info=recurly.BillingInfo())).save()
    ...: except recurly.ValidationError as err:
    ...:     pass
    ...:     

In [99]: print(err)
not_a_number: subscription.unit_amount_in_cents is not a number; invalid: subscription.plan_code is invalid; required: subscription.account.billing_info.number is required; empty: subscription.account.billing_info.address1 can't be empty; blank: subscription.account.billing_info.last_name can't be blank; blank: subscription.account.billing_info.first_name can't be blank; empty: subscription.account.billing_info.country can't be empty; empty: subscription.account.billing_info.zip can't be empty; empty: subscription.account.billing_info.city can't be empty; invalid: subscription.account.account_code is invalid

In [100]: err.errors
Out[100]: 
{'subscription.account.account_code': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b0ece8c90>,
 'subscription.account.billing_info.address1': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b1c161cd0>,
 'subscription.account.billing_info.city': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b1c161590>,
 'subscription.account.billing_info.country': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b1c161850>,
 'subscription.account.billing_info.first_name': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b1c161390>,
 'subscription.account.billing_info.last_name': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b1c161190>,
 'subscription.account.billing_info.number': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b0ece8290>,
 'subscription.account.billing_info.zip': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b0ece8dd0>,
 'subscription.plan_code': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b0ece8810>,
 'subscription.unit_amount_in_cents': <recurly.errors.Suberror at 0x7f0b0ece8fd0>}

```
We see that we only have one ```subscription.account.account_code``` instead of two

Could we have a list of suberrors as a value of the dict instead of just one suberror ?

The code is located here: https://github.com/recurly/recurly-client-python/blob/master/recurly/errors.py#L238
```python
suberrors = dict()
for err in self.response_doc.findall('error'):
    field = err.attrib['field']
    symbol = err.attrib['symbol']
    message = err.text

    suberrors[field] = self.Suberror(field, symbol, message)

self.__dict__['errors'] = suberrors
```
Maybe use a defaultdict(list) instead of a dict ?

issue_title: Object mismatch with pymongo FixedOffset
issue_body:
I've discovered that the round-trip serialization/deserialization of a simple FixedOffset object produces a corrupted object. The output of [this test script](https://gist.github.com/jaraco/d979a558bc0bf2194c23/079efd37a641159c0c14a1ab93f7efce30bf3eda) produces:

```
============================= test session starts =============================
platform win32 -- Python 3.4.2 -- py-1.4.26 -- pytest-2.6.4
collected 1 items

test_fo.py F

================================== FAILURES ===================================
________________________ test_FixedOffsetSerializable _________________________

    def test_FixedOffsetSerializable():
    	import jsonpickle
    	import bson.tz_util
    	fo = bson.tz_util.FixedOffset(-60*5, 'EST')
    	serialized = jsonpickle.dumps(fo)
    	pprint.pprint(json.loads(serialized))
    	restored = jsonpickle.loads(serialized)
    	print(restored._FixedOffset__offset)
>   	assert restored == fo
E    assert <bson.tz_util.FixedOffset object at 0x0000000005F494A8> == <bson.tz_util.FixedOffset object at 0x0000000005EEFA90>

test_fo.py:16: AssertionError
---------------------------- Captured stdout call -----------------------------
{'_FixedOffset__name': 'EST',
 '_FixedOffset__offset': {'py/id': 1},
 'py/initargs': {'py/tuple': [{'__reduce__': [{'py/type': 'datetime.timedelta'},
                                              {'py/tuple': [-1, 68400, 0]}],
                               'py/object': 'datetime.timedelta'},
                              'EST']},
 'py/object': 'bson.tz_util.FixedOffset'}
<bson.tz_util.FixedOffset object at 0x0000000005F494A8>
========================== 1 failed in 0.52 seconds ===========================
```

As you can see, the object produced isn't equal to the one serialized. Furthermore, it appears as if the `.__offset` property of the object has been set to itself instead of the timedelta object.

issue_title: Support for pickle and copy
Custom date interval behavior confusing
issue_body:
The `Year`, `Week`, `Month` and `Day` date interval types all add one extra day to the end date so that the date interval is inclusive on the beginning date and exclusive on the end date.  This makes a lot of sense and lets me easily write code like this:

```python
class KPIReport(luigi.Task):
    date_interval = luigi.DateIntervalParameter()

    def run(self):
        start = datetime.datetime.combine(self.date_interval.date_a, datetime.time())
        end = datetime.datetime.combine(self.date_interval.date_b, datetime.time())
        num_members = User.find({'signup_time': {'$gte': start, '$lt': end}}) 
        # etc...
```
So now if I run ```python reports.py KPIReport --date-interval 2015-03-20``` everything works out and I get a report for that single day.

However now if I want to run the same report on an arbitrary range, such as the last 7 days, I might think I can simply run ```python reports.py KPIReport --date-interval 2015-03-14-2015-03-20```.  But now instead of ending at 23:59:59 on the 20th my date interval ends at 23:59:59 on the 19th.  

It seems to me that the `Custom` date interval should add one day to the end date just as all of the other implementations do, or is there a different best practice for this scenario?

issue_title: Ignore JSON content type if request is HTTP GET
issue_body:
A JSON body on a GET request has no contextual meaning, but is technically allowed. In doctor if you hit a GET handler and have content-type set to application/json it currently causes a 500 error which gets re-raised by doctor.

We should instead ignore and not try to load the json body for a GET request instead of raising an error.

My test task is just a test that returns True because when I restarting the worker and beat processes the following happened:

```
[2017-09-13 16:50:12,901: INFO/MainProcess] Scheduler: Sending due task permit test (travelpermits.tasks.permit_test)

```

Hundreds upon hundreds of tasks. I killed the beat process and before I could open another command window and type out `celery purge -A django_forms` there were `Purged 2587 messages from 1 known task queue.`

issue_title: Security group authorize_* methods UserIdGroupPairs GroupName only uses default VPC
issue_body:
The security group authorize_ingress and authorize_egress methods with a specified UserIdGroupPairs GroupName only look for that GroupName in the default VPC instead of looking in the VPC of the calling security group resource. Also, specifying the UserIdGroupPairs VpcId does not have any effect.

For example:
```
> s = boto3.Session()
> ec2 = s.resource('ec2')
> vpc1 = ec2.Vpc(id='vpc-xxxxxxx1') #default VPC
> vpc2 = ec2.Vpc(id='vpc-xxxxxxx2') #VPC2
> sg1_vpc2 = ec2.SecurityGroup(id='sg-xxxxxxx1') #security group in VPC2
> new_rule1 =
{'FromPort': 25,
 'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
 'IpRanges': [],
 'PrefixListIds': [],
 'ToPort': 25,
 'UserIdGroupPairs': [{'GroupId': 'sg-xxxxxxx2'}]} #id in VPC2
> sg1_vpc2.authorize_ingress(IpPermissions=[new_rule1]) #by id -> works
> new_rule2 = 
{'FromPort': 25,
 'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
 'IpRanges': [],
 'PrefixListIds': [],
 'ToPort': 25,
 'UserIdGroupPairs': [{'GroupName': 'sg2_vpc2'}]}
> sg1_vpc2.authorize_ingress(IpPermissions=[new_rule2]) #by group name
...
ClientError: An error occurred (InvalidGroup.NotFound) when calling the AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress operation: You have specified two resources that belong to different networks.
# Error -> looking for 'sg2_vpc2' in the default vpc instead of VPC2
> new_rule3 = 
{'FromPort': 25,
 'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
 'IpRanges': [],
 'PrefixListIds': [],
 'ToPort': 25,
 'UserIdGroupPairs': [{'GroupName': 'sg2_vpc2', 'VpcId': 'vpc-xxxxxxx2'}]}
> sg1_vpc2.authorize_ingress(IpPermissions=[new_rule3]) #by group name with explicit VpcId
...
ClientError: An error occurred (InvalidGroup.NotFound) when calling the AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress operation: You have specified two resources that belong to different networks.
# Error -> looking for 'sg2_vpc2' in the default vpc instead of VPC2
```

issue_title: A resource with no tags should return an emtpy list, not NoneType, for the tags property
issue_body:
Hey there, so more growing pains from switching from boto.

When iterating over a bunch of AMIs, I have some logic in there that does this:
````python
    name = ''
    tags = {}
    #so it turns out that if a resource has no tags, it's not even an empty list, but the tags property is "None"
    #Which means if you do this, you get a TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
    for tag in ami.tags:
        if tag['Key'] == 'Name':
            name = tag['Value']
        tags[tag['Key']] = tag['Value']

````

Because I can't just reference tags like a dict like I can in boto (but that's a different story).

But if the AMI has no tags, I get
````
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
````
So in boto3, please *at least* return an empty list for <someresource>.tags, rather than making me surround this logic in *yet another layer* of conditional logic that checks to see if there even are any tags.

issue_title: One second delay with S3 upload
issue_body:
I see a 1 second delay in doing put to S3. With the help of strace, I think I have narrowed it down to an offending 'select' call:

sendto(4, "PUT /rambuck/backup/1 HTTP/1.1\r\n"..., 352, 0, NULL, 0) = 352
select(5, [4], [], [4], {1, 0})         = 0 (Timeout)
poll([{fd=4, events=POLLOUT}], 1, 60000) = 1 ([{fd=4, revents=POLLOUT}])
sendto(4, "#!/usr/bin/env python\n\nPROJECT ="..., 1249, 0, NULL, 0) = 1249

The above 'select' is waiting for POLLIN events when there is nothing to be received. I believe boto/python sends HTTP headers in the first sendto followed by another sendto for user data (in this case it is a python source file of 1249 bytes). The 'select' call should be passing writefds if it expects a POLLOUT, it instead passes readfds. 

This bug is affecting all uploads to S3 for me with a 1 second delay even for small uploads.

| Action                                         | C function         | Time spent | Percentage |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ---------- | ---------- |
| Converting the input Python string to C string | `PyArg_ParseTuple` | 0.24s      | 21%        |
| Creating the timedelta for the timezone object | `new_delta_ex`     | 0.34s      | 30%        |
| Creating the timezone object for the datetime  | `new_timezone`     | 0.15s      | 13%        |
| Creating the final datetime                    | `new_datetime_ex`  | 0.25s      | 22%        |
| Rest of computation                            |                    | 0.16s      | 14%        |

## Caching timezones

If we were to cache the timezone objects as we created them, then subsequent parses with the same timezone information would save that time.
This eliminates the expensive call to `new_delta_ex`, as well as the less expensive call to `new_timezone`. These are 43% of the run time.

I assert that in most use cases, only a handlful of offsets are ever used, so the performance would be much better after the first few parses. 

There are only `((1440 - 1) * 2) + 1 = 2879` valid offsets in ISO 8601 (that Python supports), so we can also bound the maximum amount of memory used.

issue_title: Stop stringifying None in clone kwargs
issue_body:
Kwargs to `git.Repo.clone` and `git.Repo.clone_from` with `None` values get cast to strings and passed to the git command. This leads to behavior I'd consider non-Pythonic as it's exposing a Python internal null value to something which has no understanding of it.

For example, `git.Repo.clone_from(url, path, mirror=None)` results `git clone --mirror=None -v url path`. This is not valid as `--mirror` takes no arguments.

Instead, a `None` value should result in one of two things in the git command:

- Exclude the kwarg entirely
- Include the kwarg, but treat it as a flag without arguments (ex. `--mirror` without the `=`).

issue_title: Monitoring programs specified with group
issue_body:
Hello, I believe I've found a bug in `memmon` plugin.
For a `program` argument in `memmon` call, it's [said](https://superlance.readthedocs.org/en/latest/memmon.html#command-line-syntax) that it's possible to specify group name to avoid ambiguity, with the format `group_name:program_name`. However, the documentation [placed in the code](https://github.com/Supervisor/superlance/blob/master/superlance/memmon.py#L43) suggests otherwise - `process_name:group_name`. At last I figured out that the first variant is the correct one.

When I tried to use `memmon` plugin for my program, I noticed that the following exception had been emmitted to the eventlistener log:
```
Checking programs app:backend-0=83886080
RSS of app:backend-0 is 79663104
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/vagrant/test-superlance/bin/memmon", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/home/vagrant/test-superlance/lib/python2.7/site-packages/superlance/memmon.py", line 402, in main
    memmon.runforever()
  File "/home/vagrant/test-superlance/lib/python2.7/site-packages/superlance/memmon.py", line 169, in runforever
    if  rss > self.programs[name]:
KeyError: 'backend-0'
```

As far as I've understood, this is [the line of code](https://github.com/Supervisor/superlance/blob/master/superlance/memmon.py#L173) which throws an error because the program is being looked up without group name.

To reproduce the issue, I've written an extra test:
```python
def test_runforever_tick_program_with_group(self):
    programs = {'foo:foo': 0 }
    groups = {}
    _any = None
    memmon = self._makeOnePopulated(programs, groups, _any)
    memmon.stdin.write('eventname:TICK len:0\n')
    memmon.stdin.seek(0)
    memmon.runforever(test=True)
    lines = memmon.stderr.getvalue().split('\n')
    self.assertEqual(len(lines), 4)
    self.assertEqual(lines[0], 'Checking programs foo:foo=0')
    self.assertEqual(lines[1], 'RSS of foo:foo is 2264064')
    self.assertEqual(lines[2], 'Restarting foo:foo')
    self.assertEqual(lines[3], '')
```

The fix is quite simple, I can make a pull request, if you like.

issue_title: safedelete fails to cascade properly with inheritance
issue_body:
Somewhat related to https://github.com/makinacorpus/django-safedelete/issues/55

While figuring out how to add a db index to help with query performance (I know it was added in https://github.com/makinacorpus/django-safedelete/pull/67, but only for the 0.3.x branch), I decided to subclass the SafeDeleteMixin.

```
# Override safedelete mixin to enable db index.
class CustomSafeDeleteModel(SafeDeleteMixin):
    deleted = models.DateTimeField(editable=False, null=True, db_index=True)

    class Meta:
        abstract = True
```

The culprit appears to be that it only checks the first-level of `__bases__` to see if a model is a safedelete model: https://github.com/makinacorpus/django-safedelete/blob/6a20488785d4ac335d96031204064c53a25127e5/safedelete/models.py#L10

While my current issue can be solved in other ways, this does prevent django abstract model inheritance from working properly. It only manifests itself when setting the `_safedelete_policy` to `safedelete.models.SOFT_DELETE_CASCADE`, so it's pretty subtle.

For anyone looking for a quick short-term hack around this, you can do this so that `is_safedelete` works properly:

```
# Override safedelete mixin to enable db index.
class CustomSafeDeleteMixin(object):
    deleted = models.DateTimeField(editable=False, null=True, db_index=True)

    class Meta:
        abstract = True

class MyModel(SafeDeleteMixin, CustomSafeDeleteMixin):
   # ...
```

issue_title: `pipenv run` does *not* ensure required packages are installed

    pipenv run python myprog.py

      File "listmycmds.py", line 19, in <module>
        import argh
    ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'argh'

When I run 

    pipenv install

    pipenv run python myprog.py

##### Steps to replicate

```sh
pipenv install -e .
pipenv uninstall .  # doesn't work
pipenv update .  # doesn't work
```

issue_title: syntax error on install of url-sourced pkg into a src directory containing parens
issue_body:
When installing a url-sourced package (`git+ssh://git@github.path/to/package.git#egg=package`) into a `src` directory that includes parens in the path, we get an error:

```
/bin/sh: -c: line 0: syntax error near unexpected token `('
```

##### Describe your environment

1. Mac High Sierra
1. Python version: 3.6.2
1. Pipenv version: 9.0.0

##### Expected result

The install should complete with no errors.

##### Actual result

```
/bin/sh: -c: line 0: syntax error near unexpected token `('
```

##### Steps to replicate

* create a project file with parens in the dirname -- `projdir="$HOME/dev/test_project(for testing)"; mkdir -p $projdir; cd $projdir; pipenv --three`
* install a git-url package -- `pipenv install git+ssh://git@github.com/behave/behave.git#egg=behave`
* expect the "unexpected token" error

issue_title: pipenv install --skip-lock broken with multiple sources
issue_body:
Considering the following Pipfile

```
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.example.com/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "internal"

[packages]
myprivatepackage = {index = "internal", version = "==1.0.0", editable = true}
uWSGI = "==2.0.17"
unittest-xml-reporting = "*"
coverage = "*"

[dev-packages]

[requires]
python_version = "2.7"
```
---

```bash
pipenv install --verbose --skip-lock                                                                                                                                                         1 ↵
Installing dependencies from Pipfile…
Installing 'uwsgi==2.0.17 --extra-index-url https://private.example.com/simple'

WARNING: Invalid requirement, parse error at "'--extra-'"
ABORTING INSTALL... You will have to reinstall any packages that failed to install.
You may have to manually run pipenv lock when you are finished.
```

```
pipenv, version 11.9.0
```

I believe this might have been introduced in https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/pull/1706

cc @techalchemy

issue_title: Encapsulate monkeypatching functionality
issue_body:
In [the monkeypatch module branch](/pypa/setuptools/tree/monkeypatch-module), I started work on pulling out the monkeypatching module to provide a clear indication of everything that setuptools patches in distutils, and to possibly provide a means for an application to opt out of the monkeypatching or even opt-in (a later consideration).

However, the [tests revealed](https://travis-ci.org/pypa/setuptools/builds/157532655) an [immediate failure in pbr](https://bugs.launchpad.net/pbr/+bug/1620153).

So I'm filing this ticket to capture the pending effort.

issue_title: Windows launcher doesn't quote the executable
issue_body:
In `run` on [line 305 in launcher.c](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/v23.1.0/launcher.c#L305), the quoted executable path is added to the args array (i.e. `*newargsp++ = quoted(ptr)`), but this is subsequently ignored by  `join_executable_and_args`. Instead it uses the unquoted executable path. Thus if Python is installed to "C:\Program Files", this will mistakenly execute "C:\Program.exe" if it exists. 

For example, here are the `executable` and `args` parameters passed to `join_executable_and_args`: 

    0:000> ?? (char *)@rcx
    char * 0x000001d6`aefbe100
     "C:\Program Files\Python35\python.exe"

    0:000> ?? ((char **)@rdx)[0]
    char * 0x000001d6`aefb4140
     ""C:\Program Files\Python35\python.exe""
    0:000> ?? ((char **)@rdx)[1]
    char * 0x000001d6`aefb9aa0
     ""C:\Program Files\Python35\Scripts\hello-script.py""

And here is the resulting command line passed to `CreateProcessA`:

    Breakpoint 1 hit
    KERNELBASE!CreateProcessA:
    00007ffb`9ef0ca00 4c8bdc          mov     r11,rsp
    0:000> ?? (char *)@rdx
    char * 0x000001d6`aefb6a30
     "C:\Program Files\Python35\python.exe "C:\Program Files\Python35\Scripts\hello-script.py""

`join_executable_and_args` should just be `join_args` since the quoted executable is already the first item in `args`.

I'd prefer that setuptools abandoned this old launcher code to instead use the [simple launcher](https://bitbucket.org/vinay.sajip/simple_launcher) from distlib, which supports Unicode, a job object, and embedded scripts. pip uses distlib's launcher for entry points in wheel packages.

issue_title: Docs are unclear that package_data cannot refer to a directory
issue_body:
`package_data` does not appear to allow you to include a directory. The documentation seems to explicitly say that it should work:

> `package_data`: Specify additional patterns to match files **and directories** that may or may not be matched by MANIFEST.in or found in source control.

In fact, it silently ignores you when you pass it a directory, and leads to much tearing-out of hair.

There is a [test case](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/9d12348e26d8c143d0b675ed147094a9e54e1c97/setuptools/tests/test_build_py.py) that makes sure directories are not copied. So the documentation seems to be incorrect, and not the implementation.

(As an added bonus, the docs also do not mention that all paths must be relative to the `package_dir`.)

issue_title: Both python 2.7.9 and 2.7.13 are installed in the latest python:2.7 image
issue_body:
We have a container based on python:2.7 running uwsgi, which has stopped working from a clean build at some point recently, with uwsgi failing to import datetime. Haven't been able to track down exactly why, but I'm heavily suspecting it's because python 2.7.9 is installed as `/usr/bin/python` and 2.7.13 is installed as `/usr/local/bin/python` and that uwsgi is somehow using the binary from one with the libraries from the other:

```
$ docker run python:2.7 /usr/bin/python --version
Python 2.7.9

$ docker run python:2.7 /usr/local/bin/python --version
Python 2.7.13
```

issue_title: Wrongly assumes implicit mapping pair when colon is missing
issue_body:
Input
```js
safeLoad("{a:a}")
```
Output
```json
{ 'a:a': null }
```

I believe the above should Error.  I cannot find anything in the spec saying the colon (`:`) is optional in a mapping.

The spec says (emphasis mine),
> "Note that the value may be completely empty **since its existence is indicated by the “:”**.

Then again [Flow Mapping Separate Values](http://www.yaml.org/spec/1.2/spec.html#id2791704) shows this omission when there are multiple are multiple entries in the map...

issue_title: Passing custom command to ShellSpoutSpec
issue_body:
Hello,

I tried to pass a custom [`command`](https://github.com/Parsely/streamparse/blob/master/streamparse/storm/spout.py#L127) with spaces to `ShellSpoutSpec`, e.g. `/usr/local/bin/python -m streamparse.run`. But it seems that `command` does not support spaced commands and exits on the supervisor with the following error:

`java.io.IOException: Cannot run program "/usr/local/bin/python -m streamparse.run" (in directory "/opt/storm/storm-local/supervisor/stormdist/test-2-1470753751/resources"): error=2, No such file or directory`

My intention for doing so is to distribute streamparse as part of my JAR with `pip install -t src`. Since all our code is self-contained and dockerized we can not and do not want to rely on virtualenv and ssh. Storm seems to execute `streamparse_run` in the root of the extracted JAR and `PYTHONPATH` is globally set to always contain `.`, so I think it might work.

Do you have any suggestions how to make storm use a streamparse contained in the JAR?

Thank you very much.

_FYI adding a custom start script to the root dir does not work because JARs do not preserve executable permissions._

issue_title: add render50
Handling of character set switching in UTF-8 mode
issue_body:
With pyte 0.6.0, the following code just leaves a B on the terminal display:
```
import pyte
screen = pyte.Screen(80, 24)
stream = pyte.Stream()
stream.attach(screen)
stream.feed('\x1b(B')
print(screen.display[0].strip())
```

Reading the code (and the associated FAQ entry), it says that the escape sequence should be ignored.
The code ignores it by not reading the last character.

However, if I run lynx in my shell (TERM=xterm, LANG=en_US.UTF-8), this sequence is sent quite a bit, leaving B in my terminal.

issue_title: Enhancement of peak detection features
issue_body:
Following this [thread on the mailing list](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/scikit-image/2018-April/005514.html), I initiate this issue to enhance the peak detection in scikit image.

Currently, we have two functions:
* [skimage.morphology.local_maxima](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/api/skimage.morphology.html#skimage.morphology.local_maxima)
* [skimage.feature.peak_local_max](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/api/skimage.feature.html#peak-local-max)

In particular, `peak_local_max` (and derivatives) is used in scikit-image at different places.

Our functions seem to be sub-optimal and lead to questionable peak detection, especially for plateaus as pointed out on the mailing list.


Recently, @lagru committed to scipy https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/8264 a new function to detect peaks based on their prominence. This function is particularly easy to use and efficient for 1D data. Then, it would be worth to consider whether it can be generalized to 2D data. @lagru, would you like to share your expertise on this and eventually contribute to scikit-image if you wish and if it makes sense to you?

Thanks.

issue_title: REST & TCP/UPD encoder
issue_body:
Allow libraries that can send messages over TCP/UDP/REST to interface with nupic via this encoder. 
The encoder would be instantiated by 2 URL addresses (for send/receive, en/de-code) and mode (active/passive) either the `encode()` is called, or a packet is received on the listening URI. 

Q1: do you think this is useful and would like it in nupic upstream? @rhyolight 
Q2: should TCP/UDP and REST be included together? (currently I have usecase for TCP, but REST would be very similar and can be useful eg for the cortical.io encoder) @scottpurdy @oxtopus 
Q3: this would require a new python lib as a dependency, should the idea of `encoders/extra` be revisited? *) 


*) I still think `encoders/` should be "pure mathematical" encoders that encode numbers using different encodings (Adaptive, Scalar, RDSE). "Specific" encoders (like this one, geo-*, maybe Date) should be in `encoders/extra` 

EDIT (old): ---------------------------------------------------
Would it be good idea to have REST encoder? 

It would be initialized with a url and list [parameters], for encoding it'd take 
list of [pramameter's values] in order of parameters above. 

The decode bould be tricky, so this will be an abstract class and decode must be handeled by specific use-cases. Max we could provide parser from xml/json to dict for ease. 

This could be applied to @rhyolight  's NLP CEPT code. And as a next step, a WordEncoder using CEPT is created. 

See https://github.com/numenta/nupic/issues/430 for where would this belong

issue_body:
As [discussed on the hackers' mailing list](http://lists.numenta.org/pipermail/nupic-hackers_lists.numenta.org/2015-February/003270.html).

issue_title: The SDRClassifier doesn't serialize its "maxSteps"
issue_body:
The proto declares a `maxSteps` https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/064f8b1ef003d5ee07405cd5ac41583f83ab1d35/src/nupic/proto/SdrClassifier.capnp#L11

But the Python class never writes to it: https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/76e5b07b35764826b34221104e9c43704138b9e3/src/nupic/algorithms/sdr_classifier.py#L406

One acceptable fix would be to remove `maxSteps` from the proto. It's redundant: it's just the maximum element with the `steps`, which is also serialized.

issue_title: models: use UUID for community ID and move the current ID to a separate field
issue_body:
As discussed IRL with @lnielsen and during DR meeting.

Why:
-----
**Reason 1:**
Current design require that every reference needs to be updated when the community is renamed.

**Reason 2**
B2Share would like to use this module. It is quite probable that it will have to support the migration of a community from one server to another. In order to avoid conflicts it would be needed to have unique identifiers for Communities.

**Reason to keep a user friendly id in a separate field:**
Zenodo has an endpoint for each community. Thus it needs a unique human friendly name for each community.

Who needs it:
----------------
- Zenodo for reason 1
- B2Share for reasons 1 and 2

issue_title: A better url builder
issue_body:
Hello,

I noticed that each data reader have it's own way to build URL.

That's sometimes very ugly.

For example there is things like (`yahoo/components.py`)

    idx_mod = idx_sym.replace('^', '@%5E')

the use of `urlencode` should probably be prefered

`pylint` doesn't like in `yahoo/actions.py` (tabulations)

    url = (_URL + 's=%s' % symbol + \
                '&a=%s' % (start.month - 1) + \
                '&b=%s' % start.day + \
                '&c=%s' % start.year + \
                '&d=%s' % (end.month - 1) + \
                '&e=%s' % end.day + \
                '&f=%s' % end.year + \
                '&g=v')

and many others... (a global lint would be required)

Maybe we should add in `utils` a function to build url from a base URL (and endpoint) and a dictionary of parameters and encourage each data reader developer to use it.

Such a function could be

    def _encode_url(url, params):
        """
        Return encoded url with parameters
        """
        s_params = urlencode(params)
        if len(s_params)!=0:
            return url + '?' + s_params
        else:
            return url

So in `google/daily.py` we could have

    params = {
        'q': sym,
        'startdate': start.strftime('%b %d, %Y'),
        'enddate': end.strftime('%b %d, %Y'),
        'output': "csv"
    }
    url = _encode_url(_URL, params)
    return _retry_read_url(url, retry_count, pause, 'Google')

instead of

    url = "%s%s" % (_URL,
                    urlencode({"q": sym,
                               "startdate": start.strftime('%b %d, ' '%Y'),
                               "enddate": end.strftime('%b %d, %Y'),
                               "output": "csv"}))

(I think `'%b %d, ' '%Y'` is a typo it should be `'%b %d, %Y'`)

or in `yahoo/daily.py` instead of

    url = (_URL + 's=%s' % sym +
           '&a=%s' % (start.month - 1) +
           '&b=%s' % start.day +
           '&c=%s' % start.year +
           '&d=%s' % (end.month - 1) +
           '&e=%s' % end.day +
           '&f=%s' % end.year +
           '&g=%s' % interval +
           '&ignore=.csv')

we could have

    params = {
        's': sym,
        'a': start.month - 1,
        'b': start.day,
        'c': start.year,
        'd': end.month - 1,
        'e': end.day,
        'f': end.year,
        'g': interval,
        'ignore': '.csv'
    }
    url = _encode_url(_URL, params)


WorldBank API url create could also be improved:

in `_get_data`, instead of

    url = ("http://api.worldbank.org/countries/" + countries + "/indicators/" +
           indicator + "?date=" + str(start) + ":" + str(end) +
           "&per_page=25000&format=json")

"http://api.worldbank.org" is often repeat

in such a url there is 3 parts:

base url: "http://api.worldbank.org"
endpoint: "/countries/US/indicators/NY.GNS.ICTR.GN.ZS" (for example)
parameters: date=2012:2015&per_page=25000&format=json

We shouldn't repeat ourself
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Don%27t_repeat_yourself

we could have

global

    _BASE_URL = 'http://api.worldbank.org'

in `get_data`

        endpoint = "/countries/{countries}/indicators/{indicator}"\
            .format(countries=countries, indicator=indicator)
        params = {
            'date': "%s:%s" % (start, end),
            'per_page': 25000,
            'format': 'json'
        }
        url = _encode_url(_BASE_URL + endpoint, params)

Maybe we might define how data reader "should" build URLs.

Kind regards

issue_title: The amount is higher than the maximum.
issue_body:
I'm trying to do a subscription based billing and everything is working fine, up to a point when I actually do call create subscription. Customer is even properly charged on `first` payment, but when trying to create a subscription, error occurs.

From what I have been able to figure it out, this error shows on `creditcard` type of payment when `amount` is above `250.00`, but when I call `$mollie->methods->get('creditcard')`, I see that the maximum is `1000.00`.

So, why this error?

Some code:
```
$customer=$mollie->customers->create([
	'name'=>'Test User',
	'email'=>'test@example.com,
	'metadata'=>array(
		'user_id'=>1
	)
]);
```

```
$mollie->customers_payments->withParentId($customer->id)->create([
	'amount'=>360.00,
	'description'=>'Subscription',
	'redirectUrl'=>'http://www.example.com/return-url/?with=params',
	'recurringType'=>'first',
	'metadata'=>array(
		'user_id'=>$_SESSION['uID']
	),
	'webhookUrl'=>'http://www.example.com/payment-hook/'
]);
```
Note that this payments goes through normally. User is redirected and he/she gives a consent for futher billing.

Next a check for `valid` or `pending` mandate is made, so I will skip this part of code and go straight to subscription creation:
```
$mollie->customers_subscriptions->withParentId($customer->id)->create([
	'amount'=>360.00, // anything above 250.00 throw error (The amount is higher than the maximum.)

issue_title: demos for cosmosCatalog don't apply any cuts
issue_body:
The demos that use `cosmosCatalog` do not apply any cuts on the size or flux of the galaxies.

I think it would be useful to include cuts in the demos to instruct end users.

In the deeper catalog, there is clear evidence of measurement issues that result in oddities such as zero size and zero flux.  These would not be present in an actual sample at the intended depth.

Also, the size distribution "rolls off" at the small end rather than continuing the power law trend one would expect in a real sample.  Essentially this is an additional selection effect that the end user is probably not expecting.

Mike pointed out that these cuts can be applied in the constructor.

issue_title: Allow user to force bound on scores
issue_body:
Reported by @elisabettai.
Some features (e.g. inv_time_to_first_spike when first spike is very close to start of stimulus) can create very very high scores. This confuses the algorithm.
At the moment scores that don't generate an error are not bounded (when an error occurs the score is set to 250 though).
Option should be added to EFelFeature to allow the user to force a bound on the scores, and to allow to set the value of the bound.

issue_title: New .far class selector breaks all our documents.
issue_body:
We have hundreds of documents for a computer course as well as web sites, all using Font Awesome 4.7. We have two classes `near` and `far` to flow a sidebar to the left or right. (We don't use "left" and "right" because that assumes LTR rendering; "near" and "far" make sense even with RTL languages such as Arabic or Hebrew.)

I spent last weekend upgrading to Font Awesome 5. I didn't realize the implications of Font Awesome 5 adding two more global selectors, `far` and `fas`. This completely screws up the fonts of our "far"-floated sidebars.

It is very dangerous to start defining global selectors, especially those with meaning. If Font Awesome 5 is going to start defining extra words, it should have required them to be _in conjuction_ with `fa`.

Now I'm in quite a fix. What can I do? How can I turn off Font Awesome's `far` selector and only use turn it on for certain inline elements, like `span` or `i`?

issue_title: SyntaxError thrown by InternetExplorer on detached nodes
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

We have several cases when Internet Explorer 11 throws a "SyntaxError" on some elements, inside the _fontawesome-all.js_ in the _onTree_ function at ` var candidates = toArray(root.querySelectorAll(prefixesDomQuery));`. More precisely, the error is thrown by `querySelectorAll` function. After some investigation we found that the specific nodes for which the error is thrown are not part of the DOM anymore:  `document.body.contains(root)` returns `false`, i.e. nodes that have been apparently removed.

The actual problem is that Internet Explorer does not accept pseudo selectors inside `querySelectorAll` on elements which are not part of the DOM. This IE11 behavior can be tested with the following snippet (I'm using `:not()` here as it's the pseudo element using by `prefixesDomQuery`, although any pseudo selector will trigger the exception): 

```
const el = document.createElement("div");
el.querySelectorAll(".x:not(.y)");
```

Here's a [live example on JSFiddle](https://jsfiddle.net/vvLpfym8/). Please open the developer tools first.


### What did you expect?

Since this seems to be an IE11 bug, we'd expect that (mutated) elements that are not attached to the DOM to not be processed. A possible fix would be to add a check (`DOCUMENT.body.contains(mutationRecord.target)`) inside the `forEach` in the MutationObserver callback (`observe` function).

### What version and implementation are you using?

Version: 5.0.6
Browser and version: Internet Explorer, tested on 11.850.15063 & 11.125.16299

- [x] SVG with JS
- [ ] Web Fonts with CSS
- [ ] SVG Sprites
- [ ] On the Desktop

### Reproducible test case

It's complicated... The nodes being affected are mutated by 3rd party libraries (e.g. Kendo UI) and we haven't been able to separately reproduce the problem in a lightweight example. Please note that the problem appears in at least two separate places, with the exact same symptoms: the DOM node being processed inside the MutationObserver callback is not attached to the DOM (parent node is null and `document.body.contains` returns false) and it is different from the "actual" node present in the DOM (different references). I don't know if it's normal behavior or not, but the mutation record target does not list children, although they are present in the "actual" DOM node.

However, what we can reproduce is the "SyntaxError" thrown by InternetExplorer, as described above.

### Bug report checklist

- [x] I have filled out as much of the above information as I can
- [ ] I have included a test case because my odds go _way_ up that the team can fix this when I do
- [x] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate

issue_title: FA 5 Pro difference in naming font cannot allow switch between css+webfont and svg+js
issue_body:
FA 5 Pro difference in naming font cannot allow switch between css+webfont and svg+js

For example to use it in pseudo for css-webfonts, require:

`font-family: 'Font Awesome 5 Pro'; `  and here is important font-weight: 300 or 900;

To use it in pseudo for svg+js, require:

`font-family: 'Font Awesome 5 Pro Light';`  and here doesn't matter font-weight: 300 or 900; If want filled icon is necessary to set font `font-family: 'Font Awesome 5 Pro Solid';`

Too many names for font-family. On v4 was named simple FontAwsome. It's better to have same  name and in v5.

issue_title: Feature request: Provide Ability to Disable Font Awesome CSS Injection in SVG with JS
issue_body:
### Describe the problem you'd like to see solved or task you'd like to see made easier

I would like to be able to use the JS SVG replacement without having CSS injected into the page.

This makes is more complicated to use the SVG replacement in some of my projects because of having to override much of the styling that is injected.

### Is this in relation to an existing part of Font Awesome or something new?

Yes Font-Awesome 5 SVG with JS lib

### What is 1 thing that we can do when building this feature that will guarantee that it is awesome?

Make it easy to find the documentation on how to disable the CSS injection.

### Why would other Font Awesome users care about this?

Other users may likewise want to use the SVG replacement but may find it more complicated to integrate in their projects since the CSS cannot be treated simply as a reference and instead has to be overridden.

### On a scale of 1 (sometime in the future) to 10 (absolutely right now), how soon would you recommend we make this feature?

10. It's a very small change.

### Feature request checklist

- [X] This is a single feature (i.e. not a re-write of all of Font Awesome)
- [X] The title starts with "Feature request: " and is followed by a clear feature name (Ex: `Feature request: moar cowbell`)
- [X] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome-Pro/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate

issue_title: import from mpi4py is causing python to crash on login nodes
issue_body:
The following mpi4py import on fancylogger is causing python to crash on login nodes (where we don't have a working MPI subsystem):
```
from mpi4py import MPI
```
The author of mpi4py suggested a couple of workarounds, the first being to precede the import with: 

```
import mpi4py.rc
mpi4py.rc.initialize = False
``` 

This would fix the crash on the login nodes, but I suppose the following call to `MPI.COMM_WORLD.Get_rank()` would stop working on the compute nodes.

For keeping track of the discussion, I'm also pasting here another proposed solution by the author of mpi4py: 

> However, you could write your code this way:
>
>from mpi4py import MPI
>if MPI.Is_initialized():
>    rank_id = MPI.COMM_WORLD.Get_rank()
>else:
>    rank_id = 0 # or None, and special case None values elsewhere in the logger code

Here we have some clues on how to fix the problem, however it is not clear to me how we can fix that in a way that it works on both compute and login nodes.

Any ideas?

issue_title: Log rotation failure originating from fancylogger
issue_body:
The log rotation functionality in fancylogger appears to be broken.  When log files are rotated, the existing writer ends up with a stale file handle.

For example, this appears as a log rotation failure issue in EasyBuild:
https://github.com/easybuilders/easybuild-framework/issues/1094

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.7/logging/handlers.py", line 77, in emit
    self.doRollover()
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.7/logging/handlers.py", line 140, in doRollover
    os.rename(self.baseFilename, dfn)
OSError: [Errno 13] Permission denied
```

Changing line:
>    178: MAX_BYTES = 100 * 1024 * 1024  # max bytes in a file with rotating file handler```

to:
>    178: MAX_BYTES = 0

Is a hacky, but effective fix.

issue_title: 希望能支持异步上传，多个文件同时上传
The great connection cleanup
issue_body:
I'd like to rationalize how we track, store, and use postgres database connections.  Currently the methods of passing connection information is quite haphazard, resulting in strange and unpredictable issues with hba and passwords.  It also makes it really hard to follow the code, or to make simple fixes like issue #61.

I need to get agreement from all contributors on the way to address this, because otherwise we're headed for merge conflict hell, since touching connections and connection-passing will touch quite a bit of code.

Here is my thinking on how we should do this:

* all connection information should be stored and passed as postgresql URIs.
* we should only be using two types of connections to databases, which should have sensible defaults.

The two types of connections are:

1. connections from the patroni daemon to the local PostgreSQL, which should default to a local socket connection as the supplied superuser.
2. connections from one patroni node to another as the replication user, which connection information is supplied by the key in the DCS.

All other connections should go through the API; there is no reason why a patroni node should need to connect directly to another node for any purpose other than replication (and replication support).

This would also have some other, follow-on changes:

* the URIs we are using will need to be fixed to be valid PostgreSQL URIs.  particularly, the "database" key needs to go (proper key is "dbname").
* the replication user will need perms for setting up replication slots, etc.

So ... feedback/changes/etc.?

issue_title: Complain when given a non-existing filename
issue_body:
Right now if you supply a path that doesn't exist to patronictl it will come eventually to config.py:_load_config_file that gobbles all errors coming from trying to open a non-existing filename, returning you nothing (you will get an error next saying that no available DCS methods are configured, which increases the user confusion for this particular situtation).

We should complain right away if the user told us to read the configuration from a non-existing file, possibly aborting the whole thing.

Seen at  https://github.com/zalando/patroni/issues/736

issue_title: OAuth: When verifying token, do not pass the access_token as query param
issue_body:
In decorators/security.py - verify_oauth, a session.get() call is made to the token_info_url and the access_token to be verified is passed as a query parameter.  According to the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework: Bearer Token Usage, section 5.3 ( https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-5.3 ):

> Don't pass bearer tokens in page URLs:  Bearer tokens SHOULD NOT be
      passed in page URLs (for example, as query string parameters).
      Instead, bearer tokens SHOULD be passed in HTTP message headers or
      message bodies for which confidentiality measures are taken.
      Browsers, web servers, and other software may not adequately
      secure URLs in the browser history, web server logs, and other
      data structures.  If bearer tokens are passed in page URLs,
      attackers might be able to steal them from the history data, logs,
      or other unsecured locations.

I propose that this code is changed to something along these lines:

`token_request = session.post(token_info_url, json={'access_token': token}, timeout=5)`

issue_title: package_meta tries to run git on module load (also, fails in python 2.7 if "git branch" fails)
issue_body:
I'm surprised that importing the jira client now runs `git branch` in a subprocess seemingly irrespective of being in development or not. This seems like a high performance cost to be paying at module import when not running in development. This is the case on the `develop` branch.

Also, `run_but_ignore_errors` fails in python 2.7 when there is an error in the `try` section:

```
        try:
            return subprocess.check_output(*args, shell=True, stderr=devnull,
                                           **kwargs)
        except (subprocess.SubprocessError,
                FileNotFoundError, OSError):
            return None
```

because `subprocess.SubprocessError` was introduced in Python 3.3: `AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'SubprocessError'`.

issue_title: Use python logging idioms
issue_body:
Currently, Manticore's logging uses custom logging strings, e.g. `logger = logging.getLogger('MANTICORE')`

Per the python documentation [here](https://docs.python.org/2.7/library/logging.html#logger-objects), the recommended pattern is `logging.getLogger(__name__)`. This lets us organize our logger hierarchy just like our module hierarchy.

issue_title: comment typo
Let NEXRADLevel2File's filename be a gzipped (.gz) file
issue_body:
All of the level 2 archive files that I've seen from [NOAA's data archive site](http://www.ncdc.noaa.gov/nexradinv/) have been .gz files.  Would it be worth detecting the filename's extension, and if it is a .gz file opening it with `gzip.open` instead of just using open?  As it is, you can work around this by using an open .gz file as the filename.  I can submit a pull request if I need to; it will basically only add a couple lines in `NEXRADLevel2File.__init__`:

    if filename.endswith('.gz'):  # maybe should use os.path.splitext()[-1] == '.gz' to be cross-platform?
        fh = gzip.open(filename, 'rb') # 'rb' is the default, don't really need it...

and it would need to add the relevant imports, too.

As an aside, everywhere I've seen on NOAA's website mentions that the radar data is stored as a bzipped file, but all of the ones I've seen have been gzipped.  Granted, I've only worked with a single data set from KTLX.  Are the others different?

issue_title: requests-ntlm sends POST/PUT data three times
issue_body:
HttpNtlmAuth sends data three times for POST and PUT.

The flow is following:
- a program sends a POST/PUT request
- before the request is send, HttpNtlmAuth prepares the request, sets a hook on response
- the whole request is sent, data included
- server returns 401
- HttpNtlmAuth recognizes 401 and does retry_using_http_NTLM_auth
- sends the data again
- sends the data again, this time 200 is expected

This is quite fragile and slow, especially when you try to send a huge request - hundreds of MBs to a webdav server.

issue_title: Breadcrumb variable interpolation breaking sourcemaps
issue_body:
For reference - https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass/issues/919

I think the above issue has to be reopened. Sorry if I should be posting there, kinda new to github and its issue/bug tracking.

So from what I can tell, there used to be an issue with how libsass compiled the following:

```
content: "#{$breadcrumb-separator}\00a0";
```

So it got changed to:

```
$nbsp: "\00a0";
content: "#{$breadcrumb-separator}#{$nbsp}";
```

But now that leads to it getting compiled to:

```
content: "/ ";
```

And on top of that if the libsass output style is set to `compressed` it causes source maps to be inaccurate. The other output styles are fine though. I submitted an issue over there - https://github.com/sass/libsass/issues/2319

# Proposition

Because this whole variable interpolation stuff is pretty unreliable and inconsistent across recent versions of libsass, I propose to just hardcode the value. So...

```
content: "/\00a0";
```

That way it will work for people using slightly older versions of libsass and also for people using the recent version(s). And though we won't have a simple variable to change/override, it's super easy to override the old fashion way. For example

```
.breadcrumb>li+li:before {
    content: "\003e\00a0"; // for a ">" instead of a "/"
}
```

I'd much rather have minified css and source maps than a variable for breadcrumbs. I know, as a work around, I could use a separate css minifier, but they add nearly one second to compile time. Libsass is fast and hard to beat and I really like to keep things simple and fast whenever possible.

Thoughts on this?

issue_title: Pants binary dependencies should include hashes that can be checked against public trusted hashes
issue_body:
Since pants serves up static binaries generated by others, eg: go, node and other distributions, it is imperative we can compare a hash of the binaries with the most trusted public hashes of the originals.

This PR by a pants contributor - not committer - helps highlight the concern. We should serve a go distribution as trustworthy as those served by golang and right now we allow arbitrary modification and act as a man in the middle: https://github.com/pantsbuild/binaries/pull/27

issue_title: Address package conflict case in Python Distribution backend task
issue_body:
This is a continuation of the work from https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/5141.

The `BuildLocalPythonDistributions` backend task enables users to define a `python_dist` target and pass in a setup.py that the task will run. This allows them to build wheels from Python packages that may also contain C/C++ extensions and compose them into their BUILD targets. 

A potential issue in the current implementation is that there is no treatment for resolving the transitive dependencies of a user-supplied setup.py listed in setup() fields such as `install_requires` and `setup_requires`. There is also the issue of if a user wishes to package a python dist that has the same name as a dependency listed on the consuming target. This could cause the pex to break in subtle ways if the consuming target has a dependency of the same name but different version. 

The scope of this ticket is to address these issues by extracting transitive dependencies from a user-defined setup.py and injecting them into the pex resolver used by the `resolve_requirements` backend task. The issue of user-defined package names colliding with dependencies on the consuming target can be solved by reading the name of the wheel built by the `BuildLocalPythonDistributions` backend task, extracting the package name and version from this metadata, and passing it to the pex resolver alongside the transitive dependencies extracted from the setup.py file supplied by the user.

issue_title: Pants should support generation of targets for local go src
issue_body:
The `go list` tool provides everything needed to automatically generate go_library and go_binary targets for a local src tree when combined with pants resolve support for go.  A codegen task could either (re)create real BUILD files to fill in a full graph given a target root or else it could do so purely in-memory.  Having a such a task installed either as a top-level manual task (`./pants go-buildgen`) or else as installed as a `gen` task that implements [`alternate_target_roots`](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/core/tasks/task.py#L125-L133) and does complete dynamic generation transparently in-memory would ease transition for a go developer from their already very fast and hermetic low-overhead build system.  The only targets they'd need to define on disk in the latter case are those they wanted to address using pants on the command line (only the go_binary targets might be a common choice) and the remote library direct dependencies used by go code in the repo.

This idea grew out of a fruitful brainstorming session with @huckphin and @traviscrawford supplemented with feedback from @stuhood and @slyphon.

issue_title: GoBuildgen is lossy when materializing indirect remote dep BUILDs
issue_body:
Unless those indirect remote deps are included in the initial build graph via, for example:
```
./pants buildgen.go --materialize --remote 3rdparty/golang::
```
Any rev specified in a pre-existing BUILD of an indirect (transitive) remote import path dep is lost.

This is related to #2372 which was only partially fixed as it turns out!

issue_title: Error when transforming parameter list of strings that contains numbers
issue_body:
Some strings are not transformed correctly when executing `aws cloudformation package`.

Given the following `template.yaml`:

```
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: Example Template

Parameters:
  Month:
    Description: Select month
    Type: String
    AllowedValues: ["01", "02", "03", "04", "05", "06", "07", "08", "09", "10", "11", "12"]
    Default: "06"

Resources:
  ExampleLambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      CodeUri: .
      Description: Example Lambda
      Environment:
        Variables:
          MONTH: !Ref Month
      Handler: index.handler
      Policies: AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Runtime: nodejs6.10
```

Executing `aws cloudformation package` result in the following `packaged-template.yaml`:

```
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Description: Example Template
Parameters:
  Month:
    AllowedValues:
    - '01'
    - '02'
    - '03'
    - '04'
    - '05'
    - '06'
    - '07'
    - 08
    - 09
    - '10'
    - '11'
    - '12'
    Default: '06'
    Description: Select month
    Type: String
Resources:
  ExampleLambda:
    Properties:
      CodeUri: s3://billing-deployment/360dc36bb10d9734e0e16e96cdf2d171
      Description: Example Lambda
      Environment:
        Variables:
          MONTH:
            Ref: Month
      Handler: index.handler
      Policies: AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole
      Runtime: nodejs6.10
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
```

Note that **08** and **09** are translated as numbers, not strings.

$ aws --version
aws-cli/1.11.83 Python/2.7.10 Darwin/16.5.0 botocore/1.5.46

issue_title: Shift pages
issue_body:
Hi all,

I have a word doc model with a front page, a blank middle page and a final page (with background/image and some text), all of them with a custom style.

What I want to do is to modify this doc adding middle pages but without modifying the first and last one. I can successfully do the first part, leaving the first page untouched and adding new pages, but then it ends up writing over my last page content.

Is there a way to make sure that my current last page will always be so?

issue_title: How to get and distribute freeimage libraries
issue_body:
- [x] Let setup.py make wheels containing the libs for Windows and OSX (#108)
- [ ] Get a good freeimage conda recipe accepted at anaconda-recipes
- [ ] Do a release. Get things out in the wild
- [ ] Create/update imageio conda recipe
- [ ] Add a script to imageio-binaries that downloads the freeimage libs from anaconda.org, so that we have one source for the freeimage lib (imageio-binaries is then basically a mirror). Or ... Maybe we should make imageio download from anaconda.org directly.

- [ ] Maybe we can eventually deprecate the auto-downloading?

cc @blink1073

issue_title: imageio litters /usr/local in OS X
issue_body:
imageio was automatically installed on my Mac (OS X 10.10.2) as a dependency of [moviepy](https://github.com/Zulko/moviepy) via `pip install moviepy`.

Apparently, it wrote `/usr/local/README.md` and several other files, such as `/usr/local/LICENSE`, thereby overwriting [Homebrew](http://brew.sh/)'s README, causing it to fail when upgrading. It also created `/usr/local/tests` and other directories I now have to manually clean up.

Please do not litter into `/usr/local`, but choose a more sensible place. I don't know how Python packaging works exactly, but `/usr/local` does not seem to be the place where a package should reside.

If this is an issue with moviepy, please let me know so I can post it there instead.

issue_title: Wrong instantiation of urllib2.HTTPSHandler
issue_body:
Here https://github.com/googleads/googleads-python-lib/commit/5820c980f4733606c93149e62e7a6737785eda6d#diff-16683aec6de1b227110863506f6fb267R380

`handlers.append(urllib2.HTTPSHandler(self._ssl_context))` is wrong because HTTPSHandler has `debuglevel` as first parameter

````
 class HTTPSHandler(AbstractHTTPHandler):

        def __init__(self, debuglevel=0, context=None):
            AbstractHTTPHandler.__init__(self, debuglevel)
            self._context = context
````

please correct line 380 to `handlers.append(urllib2.HTTPSHandler(context=self._ssl_context))`

Isn't this a security issue too?

issue_title: Blind increment on Counter column
issue_body:
As far as I could tell it's not possible to increment a counter column by a specified amount.  Instead, the counter value must be explicitly set.  The CounterUpdateClause simply takes the supplied value and subtracts the previous value.  I believe that an update should be able to be called on a counter column without knowing the previous value.  Something along the lines of

MyModel.objects.filter(id=1).update(counter_col__increment=1)

which result in the clause: 
"counter_col" = "counter_col" + 1

-or-

MyModel.objects.filter(id=1).update(counter_col__decrement=1)

which would result in the clause:
"counter_col" = "counter_col" - 1

In my specific use case, I usually don't care what the actual value of the counter is.  I just need to increment it.  It saves a query on the database.  This would also keep the counter thread safe since there is no guarantee that the "previous" parameter supplied to ContainerUpdateClause.__init__ is fresh.

http://www.datastax.com/documentation/cql/3.0/cql/cql_using/use_counter_t.html

issue_title: Initial composable PROC TABULATE implementation
issue_body:
(I'm dropping this in an issue for response rather working immediately towards a PR, because it might be more appropriate as an external add-on library rather than direct incorporation -- thoughts / reactions very welcome.)

I've finished an initial implementation of the PROC TABULATE procedure, opting for a highly composable syntax inspired somewhat by the query-abstraction tools of `SQLAlchemy` or `pydal` (to which I've also contributed a bit in the past). The reason for taking this approach is that our office (institutional research at a state university) very often produces deep-tabulated descriptive statistics, and is therefore a heavy user of the base TABULATE procedure on large datasets -- and while the macro language allows for some basic reuse of fragments and code, I've been looking to establish a higher level of abstraction and systematic composition of table structures.

The best overview I can give is this example notebook which shows off the current working syntax:
https://github.com/jasonphillips/saspy/blob/proc-tabulate/SAS%2BTabulate.ipynb

That branch also contains the working code. In short, I add a `tabulate` key to datasets which contains functions for generating class / var / statistic variables, and a `table` function which can then be passed a composition of those elements, generating the full table syntax from the elements and also generating the class / var statements by assembling all the classes and vars used. 

Anyhow, I built it by working backwards from "what syntax would I most enjoy as a user?", and its logic is already very solid, although I still need to implement further TABULATE options beyond the essential class / var / table statements. I'm curious to hear reactions!

issue_title: Explicitly ask for generator
issue_body:
When iterating over paginated data, only return generator if explicitly requested.   
One possible way of doing this is:
``` python
for item in resp().pages():
    print(item)
``` 
By doing this, it's also possible to  pass extra params like specify the max number of pages that should be fetched, or the maxi number of results that should be yelded:
``` python
for item in resp().pages(max_pages=2, max_items=40):
    print(item)

# in this example, the for loop will stop after two pages are fetched or 40 items are yelded, 
# witch ever comes first.
```

issue_title: Clear caches before running unit tests
issue_body:
Surprisingly, one of the unit tests started failing on my machine (only a single specific test), while it did succeed on CI. This was checking out the master branch without any changes (commit f348dd8).

It turns out that the issue is caused by the caching that is done with midi files. In this particular test (testImportChordVoiceA) a midi file is loaded from the disk, however if this file is first loaded with a buggy implementation (because one was testing out changes to the system) it will then continue to fail on all later tests even if the buggy implementation is fixed because the midi file load has been cached.

This is a highly unintuitive behavior and makes it really confusing for new users if they want to run the unit tests locally.

The best solution would be to clear the cache directory before the unit tests run.

issue_title: [Version 1.1.0] Pytest exception in thread after tests are run
issue_body:
After all my tests run, I get this output:

```
Exception in thread Thread-3:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 810, in __bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/home/ubuntu/environment/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pytest_server_fixtures/base.py", line 46, in run
    if not isinstance(l, string_types):
TypeError: isinstance() arg 2 must be a class, type, or tuple of classes and types
```

I'm using a custom fixture class I wrote to bring up postgres;

``` python
# conftest.py

@pytest.fixture(scope='session')  # pylint: disable=no-member
def postgres_server_sess(request):
    """ Postgres server with session scope
    """
    server = _postgres_server(request)
    return server

def _postgres_server(request):
    """Take a pytest fixture request and set up a postgres server for it"""
    test_server = PostgresServer()

    _getconn = mock.patch('app.connection_pool.getconn,
                          side_effect=lambda: test_server.connect('db_name'))
    _putconn = mock.patch('app.connection_pool.putconn',
                          side_effect=lambda conn: conn.close())
    _getconn.start()
    _putconn.start()
    request.addfinalizer(_getconn.stop)
    request.addfinalizer(_putconn.stop)

    # For orm session
    _connection_config = mock.patch.object(AppConfiguration, 'db_config',
                                           new_callable=PropertyMock,
                                           side_effect=lambda: dict(test_server.connection_config,
                                                                    password='',
                                                                    database='db_name'))

    _connection_config.start()
    request.addfinalizer(_connection_config.stop)

    test_server.start()
    request.addfinalizer(test_server.teardown)

    return test_server

class PostgresServer(TestServer):
    random_port = True

    def kill(self, retries=5):
        if hasattr(self, 'pid'):
            os.kill(self.pid, self.kill_signal)

    def pre_setup(self):
        (self.workspace / 'db').mkdir()  # pylint: disable=no-value-for-parameter

        self.pg_bin = subprocess.check_output(["pg_config", "--bindir"]).rstrip()
        subprocess.check_call([
            self.pg_bin + '/initdb',
            str(self.workspace / 'db')
        ])
        logger.debug("pg_bin = %s", self.pg_bin)

        self.connection_config = {
            u'host': u'localhost',
            u'user': os.environ['USER'],
            u'port': self.port,
            u'connect_timeout': 3,  # In seconds
        }
        self.delete = True

    def post_setup(self):
        set_up_schema_and_migrations()

    @property
    def run_cmd(self):
        logger.debug("Starting postgres")
        return [
            self.pg_bin + '/postgres',
            '-F',
            '-k', str(self.workspace / 'db'),
            '-D', str(self.workspace / 'db'),
            '-p', str(self.port),
            # '-c', "log_destination=stderr"
        ]

    def check_server_up(self):
        try:
            print("Connecting to Postgres at localhost:{}".format(self.port))
            with self.connect('postgres') as conn:
                conn.set_session(autocommit=True)
                with conn.cursor() as cursor:
                    cursor.execute("CREATE DATABASE db_name")
            self.connection = self.connect('db_name')
            with open(self.workspace / 'db' / 'postmaster.pid', 'r') as f:
                self.pid = int(f.readline().rstrip())
            return True
        except OperationalError as e:
            logger.warn("Could not connect to test postgres: %s", str(e))
        return False

    def connect(self, database):
        return psycopg2.connect(database=database, **self.connection_config)
```

issue_title: Remote data packages with resources relative to their URL should be safe
issue_body:
For example, the datapackage.json at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datasets/gdp/master/datapackage.json defines a resource with a relative path (`data/gdp.csv`), which should be downloaded at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datasets/gdp/master/data/gdp.csv. This should be considered safe, as their "base path" is the same (`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datasets/gdp/master`).

This currently isn't the case. This issue was reported by @ndkv's on https://github.com/ckan/ckanext-datapackager/issues/38#issuecomment-199694709

> I ran into a similar safe/unsafe issue: uploading a remote `datapackage.json` (i.e. unzipped) that uses relative `path`s for its resources [1] gives an `Unsafe ...` error while uploading the same package with "absolute" `url`s [2] works fine. 
> 
> This seems to be the intended behaviour
> 
> > To avoid a resource with a path like `/etc/shadow`, this extension only accepts paths relative to the datapackage's ZIP file. As you haven't uploaded a ZIP, but the actual datapackage.json, that doesn't exist, so no local path is valid.
> 
> While I see the need to prevent `/etc/shadow`-like paths, I'm not sure disallowing relative resource `path`s for remote packages (i.e. unzipped) is intended as well. Also, the spec allows relative `path`s for remote pacakges
> 
> > NOTE: the use of a `url` allows a data package to reference data not contained locally in the Data Package. _The `path` attribute may also be used for Data Packages located online – in this case it determines the resource file’s URL relative to the `datapackage.json`’s URL._
> 
> I'm not familiar (at all) with inner working of CKAN/extension code but it seems that the culprit is line 151 in the `safe` function linked above
> 
> ``` python
> if not resource.local_data_path.startswith(self.base_path):
> ```
> 
> I guess remote data packages have their URL as `base_path`. Relative `path`s are rejected since they don't start with the URL. `url`s on the other hand pass this test just fine. 
> 
> So, to allow relative paths on remote packages we'd need to treat `path` differently, right? 
> 
> [1] https://github.com/ndkv/datasets/blob/master/gemeenschappelijke_regelingen/partial/datapackage.json
> [2] https://github.com/ndkv/datasets/blob/gh-pages/gemeenschappelijke_regelingen/partial/datapackage.json
> [3] http://dataprotocols.org/data-packages/#required-fields-1
> 
> BTW The "unsafe" message initially tricked me into thinking that the package's `description` attribute was the culprit as it contains links (it isn't and the links are stripped). It might be better, if possible, to mention something about the difference between `url` and `path`.
